understanding.goal_understanding: The objective was to solve each level within a limited step budget by moving the selectable piece(s) around the grid to achieve the level’s goal state (typically aligning/moving pieces or blocks onto goal tiles marked with '+').
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Positions were given as pixel coordinates, with a 3-pixels-per-cell grid; each MOVE action shifted the currently selected piece exactly one cell (±3 pixels) in the chosen direction. If a pushable object (e.g., a block) was directly adjacent in the movement direction, it could be pushed and would move together with the selected piece, provided nothing blocked it. Walls ('#') and boundaries could prevent movement, though some wall-like barriers might behave differently and had to be tested; the movement_blocked flags were only heuristic hints (adjacent object present) and didn’t guarantee failure. Levels could contain multiple selectable pieces, and CLICK on a selectable’s exact coordinate switched control to that piece.
